response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a university professor and social media user, I believe that the best way to manage and deal with fake content on social media is to be proactive and critical in my consumption. Here are some steps I take to avoid falling victim to misinformation: 
Firstly, I fact-check everything I come across, especially if it seems too good (or bad) to be true. I use reputable sources such as CNN, BBC, or The New York Times to verify the accuracy of the information. 
Secondly, I am cautious about who I follow on social media. I only follow accounts that are trustworthy and have a proven track record of providing accurate information. This includes news outlets, academic journals, and experts in their respective fields. 
Thirdly, I use social media management tools such as Hootsuite or Buffer to schedule my posts and manage my time on social media more effectively. By doing so, I can avoid getting bogged down by fake content and focus on sharing valuable information that is relevant to my audience. 
Lastly, I believe it's essential to be mindful of my own biases and to seek out diverse perspectives. By being aware of my own limitations and actively seeking out different viewpoints, I can make more informed decisions and avoid being swayed by misinformation. 
In conclusion, managing and dealing with fake content on social media requires a proactive and critical approach. By fact-checking everything we come across, being cautious about who we follow, using social media management tools, and seeking out diverse perspectives, we can make social media a safer and more informative platform for everyone.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, I take a proactive and critical approach. I fact-check everything, cautiously follow trustworthy accounts, use social media management tools, and seek out diverse perspectives to avoid falling victim to misinformation.
